sections.paragraph:
ἐκλιπαρέω 1 aor. ἐξελιπάρησα (λιπαρέω ‘persevere’; Strabo et al.; Apollon. Paradox. 3; Philostrat., Vi. Apoll. 4, 1 p. 125, 17; Philo, In Flacc. 31; Jos., Ant. 5, 260) to plead with so as to persuade, beg, entreat πολλά earnestly (Diog. L. 4, 7) MPol 4.—DELG s.v. λίπτω.

aor. aor. = aorist

Strabo Strabo , I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Philostrat Philostrat = Flavius Philostratus, the Sophist II–III a.d.—List 5

Vi. Vi. = Vita, Vitae

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 306
